title: Nothing Phone 4a and 4a Pro launching on 5 March: Design, expected specs and more
summary: Nothing is set to launch its Phone 4 (a) series on 5 March. The launch event is also likely to see the unveling of new Headphone (a) with bold colors and long battery life. The Phone 4a will likely include a Snapdragon 7s Gen 4 processor, a triple camera setup along with a 6.78 inch OLED display.

title: OpenAI defends rival Anthropic against Pentagon ban, Sam Altman calls it ‘extremely scary precedent’
summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman expressed concerns over the US government's ban on rival Anthropic, labeling it a scary precedent. Despite OpenAI's past tensions with Anthropic, Altman defended the rival AI lab during a recent AMA session.

title: Did Grok predict US attack on Iran? Elon Musk claims victory for xAI after viral test
summary: &nbsp;Elon Musk's Grok chatbot uniquely predicted the date of the US strike on Iran as 28 February 2026, even prior to the attack. The prediction was made by the AI chatbot in a methodological test on 25 February.

title: Anthropic's Claude AI hits number 1 spot on App Store as users boycott OpenAI's new Pentagon deal
summary: Anthropic's Claude has jumped to the nubmer 1 spot on Apple App Store hours after being banned by the US government. Meanwhile, OpenAI's ChatGPT is once again facing calls for boycott and subscription cancellations after its deal with the US government.&nbsp;

title: Trump banned Anthropic — hours later, US military used its Claude AI in Iran strikes: Report
summary: Despite announcing a phase-out, the US used Anthropic's AI during an airstrike on Iran. The technology supported intelligence and target identification, underscoring its significance in military operations, including previous use in capturing Nicolás Maduro.

title: Amid Israel-Iran conflict, FlightRadar24 starts showing error due to ‘unprecedented’ volume
summary: Flightradar24 reported unprecedented traffic on its platform following US strikes on Iran. The company acknowledged site availability issues and stated that engineers are actively working to resolve them, thanking users for their patience.

title: Xiaomi 17 Ultra with Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 SoC, 6,330 mAh battery launched globally: Price, specs and more
summary: Xiaomi has unveiled the Xiaomi 17 Ultra in Barcelona, featuring a Leica triple camera, Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5, and a 6.9-inch OLED display. Prices start at 1,499 Euros for the base model, with IP68 rating and a 6,000mAh battery.

title: Xiaomi 17 with triple 50MP Leica cameras, 6,330mAh battery launched in India: Price, specs and more
summary: Xiaomi launched the flagship Xiaomi 17 in Barcelona, competing with Oppo Find X9 and OnePlus 15. It offers four colors, starts at 999 Euros, features a 6.3-inch OLED display, Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 processor, Leica triple camera setup, and a 6,330mAh battery with fast charging.

title: Xiaomi 17 Ultra & Pad 8 India Launch Highlights: Xiaomi 17, Xiaomi 17 Ultra, Pad 8 series launched
summary: Xiaomi India Launch 2026 Live Updates: Xiaomi has launched its Xiaomi 17 series at the Barcelona event. Both the phones come with a LEICA triple camera setup.

title: Xiaomi 17, Xiaomi 17 Ultra, Pad 8 global launch today: LIVE-STREAM details, expected specs, features and more
summary: Xiaomi is launching its flagship Xiaomi 17 series and Pad 8 tablet today. The live-stream starts at 2PM Barcelona time. The Xiaomi 17 features a 6.3-inch OLED display, Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 processor, Leica triple camera, and a 7,000mAh battery with fast charging.

title: Amazon India to remove seller fee for items under Rs 1,000 from mid-March
summary: In April 2025, Amazon India had waived the seller referral fee for items below Rs 300. Now the company has expanded its zero referral fees from 1.2 crore products in 2025 to over 12.5 crore products.

title: AI can cut animation costs by 25-40%, speed up production, says Priyank Kharge
summary: The minister was speaking at the valedictory ceremony of GAFX, a three-day conference on animation, visual effects, gaming, comics (AVGC) and extended reality held in Bengaluru.

title: TCS, Infosys defer trips to Middle East amid rising tensions, issue advisories to employees
summary: TCS has suspended all incoming and outgoing travel to and from the Middle East region, including transit routes, citing airspace closures across several countries. Infosys, meanwhile, has “strongly discouraged” employees from undertaking non-essential travel to the region until further notice.

title: X to debut tag for content creators to flag AI-generated posts
summary: The move comes as governments and regulators push platforms to get more serious about provenance and misinformation. India has directed platforms such as Facebook, Instagram, and YouTube to clearly label AI-generated content and ensure that they carry embedded identifiers.

title: What to know about the clash between the Pentagon and Anthropic over military's AI use
summary: President Donald Trump and Hegseth accused rising AI star Anthropic of endangering national security after its CEO Dario Amodei refused to back down over concerns the company's products could be used for mass surveillance or autonomous armed drones.

title: Semicon 2.0 to boost deep tech startups, create design ecosystem: Union minister Ashwini Vaishnaw
summary: India's Semicon Mission 2.0 will prioritise deep tech startups and a robust design ecosystem. The focus shifts from manufacturing to creating indigenous capabilities in semiconductor design and equipment.

summary: OpenAI’s defence deal, which followed Anthropic being labelled a supply chain risk, has intensified scrutiny of the AI industry’s ties with the military.
